themes.text: Capitale: Assunzione Popolazione: 7,52 milioni (2024) Superficie: 406.752 km2 Fuso orario: la differenza oraria con l’Italia varia da meno 4 ore a meno 5 ore a seconda dei tempi d’introduzione dell’ora legale e/o di ristabilimento dell’ora solare in Italia. Lingue: spagnolo e guaranì. Religioni: cattolica è predominante (90%). Presenti il protestantesimo ed in misura minore altre religioni settarie. Moneta: Guaranì-Gs (PYG) Prefisso per l'Italia: 00239 Prefisso dall'Italia:00595 Clima: subtropicale. L’inverno è breve (luglio-agosto). Di solito, salvo eccezioni, con temperature medie che vanno dai 12ºc a 18ºc durante il giorno e scendono anche al di sotto dei 4ºc durante la notte; in estate (novembre-aprile), la temperatura media è di oltre 33º con punte, tra dicembre e febbraio, fino a 45º-48º. Caratteristiche climatiche rilevanti sono la forte umidità (sino a 98%) e le repentine escursioni termiche anche di oltre 20º, che possono verificarsi nell'arco di poche ore. Frequenti le precipitazioni, soprattutto tra ottobre e maggio, ad esclusione della regione nord-occidentale del “Chaco”.

themes.heading: Turvallisuustaso
themes.text: Noudata tavanomaista varovaisuutta UM:n matkustustiedotteet ovat suosituksia Vastaat matkapäätöksestäsi aina itse. Paikallisilla viranomaisilla on ensisijainen vastuu kaikkien maan alueella oleskelevien ihmisten turvallisuudesta ja hätätilanteissa etsintä- ja pelastustoimista. Kriisitilanteissa noudata paikallisten viranomaisten ohjeita. Seuraa uutisointia tiedotusvälineissä sekä tiedotusta edustuston verkkosivuilla ja sosiaalisen median kanavissa. Ulkoministeriö ja Suomen ulkomaanedustustot avustavat suomalaisia konsulipalvelulain mukaisesti. Mikäli Euroopan unionin ulkopuolisessa kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, voit kääntyä muiden EU-maiden edustustojen puoleen. Matkatoimiston järjestämällä matkalla olevaa avustaa ensisijaisesti matkanjärjestäjä. Konsulipalvelulaki Konsulisuojeludirektiivi Laki matkapalveluyhdistelmistä Suosittelemme aina matkavakuutuksen ottamista. Tarkista ennen matkaasi matkavakuutuksesi kattavuus ja voimassaolo erityisesti jos sinulla on jokin sairaus, olet raskaana, matkasi suuntautuu vaarallisille alueille tai harrastat riskialttiita lajeja, Sairastuneen tai vainajan kotiuttaminen on kallista ja vaikea järjestää ilman kattavaa matkavakuutusta, eikä hoitoa aina saa ulkomailla ilman maksusitoumusta tai ennakkomaksua. Tee matkustusilmoitus osoitteessa https://matkustusilmoitus.fi/

themes.heading: Yleinen turvallisuustilanne
themes.text: Paraguayn turvallisuustilanne erityisesti pääkaupunki Asunciónissa on kohentunut, mutta matkustamista Paraguayn pohjoisosissa tulee harkita huolella mahdollisten levottomuuksien ja väkivaltaisuuksien takia. themes.heading: Rikollisuus
themes.text: Maan pääkaupunki Asunción on suhteellisen rauhallinen päiväsaikaan. Öisin esiintyy rikollisuutta, joten vältä kaupungin vanhaa keskustaa (Centro Histórico), yöelämän riskejä, liikkumista syrjäisissä kaupunginosissa ja julkisten kulkuneuvojen käyttöä. Suhtaudu varovaisesti tuntemattomien henkilöiden avunantotarjouksiin. Säilytä passi, suuret käteisvarat ja arvoesineet hotellin tallelokerossa tai vastaavassa turvallisessa paikassa. Henkilöllisyys on voitava todistaa viranomaisille pyydettäessä, joten passista otettua valokopiota on hyvä kuljettaa mukana. On suositeltavaa käyttää ainoastaan virallisten taksitoimistojen alaisuudessa toimivia autoja. Aseellisia ryöstöjä saattaa tapahtua myös päiväsaikaan jopa pääkaupungin vilkkailla ostoskaduilla. Ryöstöjä kohdistuu myös pitkän linjan busseihin mm. São Paulo – Asunción, joten tämä on syytä ottaa huomioon matkasuunnitelmaa tehdessä. Maassa esiintyy myös huumerikollisuutta, huumejengien yhteenottoja ja toimintaa erityisesti Brasilian rajaseudulla. Suomen Asunciónin-kunniakonsulaatista ei voi anoa uutta passia, vaan passihakemus on laitettava vireille suurlähetystössä esim. Buenos Airesissa. Käänny tarvittaessa Saksan suurlähetystön puoleen EDT - Emergency Travel Documentin anomista varten ja hae siihen Argentiinan viisumi, jos olet tulossa maahan. Tarkista viisumitarve myös muihin lähialueen maihin, jos haet uutta passia muualta. Jos passisi katoaa tai varastetaan, tee siitä ilmoitus paikalliselle poliisiasemalle. Mikäli kohdemaassa ei ole Suomen edustustoa, hae hätämatkustusasiakirjaa (ETD = Emergency Travel Document) toisen EU-maan edustustosta. Ota kopiot tärkeimmistä asiapapereista kuten passista ja lentolipuista. Säilytä ne erillään alkuperäisistä.

themes.heading: Liikenne
themes.text: Paraguayn tieverkosto on hyvin puutteellinen, ns. päätiet on rakennettu vain maakuntien pääkaupunkien välillä ja ne ovat usein vain yksikaistaisia. Useimmat yleiset tiet ovat päällystämättömiä, ilman valaistusta ja opasteita, joten yöllä ajamista ei suositella. Erityistä varovaisuutta on noudatettava, koska esim. nautakarja ja hevoset liikkuvat usein irti ja ilman valvontaa, mikä aiheuttaa vaaratilanteita erityisesti yöaikaan. Liikennekulttuuri Paraguayssa poikkeaa suomalaisesta, sillä liikennesääntöjä ei noudateta, ajo holtitonta ja onnettomuuksien määrä on korkea. Paraguayssa on laajoja, harvaan asuttuja alueita, joilla turvallisuustilanne on heikko ja jonne poliisivalvonta ei ulotu. Näitä alueita tulee välttää ja harkita, onko omalla tai vuokra-autolla liikkuminen turvallisin vaihtoehto. Harvaan asutuilla seuduilla teiden kunto vaihtelee, suurin osa maanteistä on hiekkateitä, ja reittivaihtoehdot kannattaa selvittää huolellisesti ennen matkaan lähtöä. Nelivetoinen maastoauto on suositeltava kulkuneuvo maaseudulla liikuttaessa.

themes.heading: Luonnonolot
themes.text: Paraguayissa ei ole tyypillisesti suuria luonnonmullistuksia. Ilmasto on leuto ympäri vuoden paitsi erittäin kuumina kesäkuukausina. Rankkasateita ja niiden aiheuttamia tulvia saattaa esiintyä, joiden seurauksena tiestöt muuttuvat osittain ajokelvottomiksi. Noudata varovaisuutta maastossa, sillä koko maassa esiintyy erittäin myrkyllisiä matelijoita.

themes.heading: Terveys
themes.text: Yksityiset sairaalat ovat yleensä hyvätasoisia. Suositus on hankkia kattava matkavakuutus matkustaessa Paraguayihin. Apteekeissa saa lääkkeitä, joita usein myydään myös ilman reseptiä. Dengue- ja chikungunya-kuumetta esiintyy koko maassa erityisesti kesäkaudella marraskuusta huhtikuulle. Molemmat ovat hyttysten (Aedes Aegypit) aiheuttamia kuumetauteja. Maassa esiintyy myös keltakuumetta (kts. maahantulorajoitukset/rokotukset), malariaa ja hepatiittia. Suojautuminen tehokkailla hyttyskarkotteilla ja suojaavilla vaatteilla on suositeltavaa. Lisätietoa: Paraguayn terveysministeriö – Ministerio de Salud Pública y Bienestar Social (es) Selvitä ennakkoon matkakohteesi tartuntatautitilanne ja rokotussuositukset. Löydät ne seuraavien viranomaisten verkkosivuilta: Matkailijan terveysopas Maailman terveysjärjestö WHO Euroopan tartuntatautivirasto

themes.heading: Maan lait ja käytännöt
themes.text: Rangaistukset huumausaineiden hallussapidosta, myynnistä ja salakuljetuksesta voivat olla Paraguayissa erittäin kovia. Huolehdi, ettei matkatavaroihisi lisätä sinulle kuulumattomia tavaroita, älä suostu kuljettamaan toisten matkatavaroita. Henkilöllisyys on pystyttävä todistamaan viranomaisille, joten pidä mukanasi passistasi kopio tai puhelimessa valokuva. Selvitä paikallisviranomaisilta ennen matkalle lähtöä tullirajoituksia koskevat tuonti- ja vientiohjeet. Paraguayn tulli “Matkailijan elektorinen tullausselvitys” / Aduana - Declaración Electrónica Aduanera de Viajeros (e-DAV) ohjeet (es, pt, eng)

themes.heading: Maahantulosäännökset
themes.text: Paraguayn maahantulo-ohjeet ulkomaalaisille: Dirección Nacioanal de Migraciones de Paraguay (es) Paraguayn viranomaiset vaativat keltakuumerokotuksen kaikilta henkilöiltä, jotka saapuvat maahan keltakuumeen riskialueilta Boliviasta, Brasiliasta, Kolumbiasta, Perusta, Ranskan Guyanasta ja muutamista Afrikan valtioista. Maahan saapuessa on esitettävä kv. rokotustodistus. Lisätietoa: Requisitos sanitarios de ingreso y salida del Paraguay (es) Maassa pysyvästi asuva alaikäinen (alle 18v) lapsi, kansalaisuudesta riippumatta tarvitsee matkustusluvan ”Autorización para viajar el exterior” matkustaessaan yksin tai muiden kuin vanhempiensa kanssa maasta ulos. Lisätietoa: Corte Suprema de Justicia (es) UM ei vastaa vieraiden valtioiden maahantulosäännöksistä, kuten passin voimassaoloa koskevista ehdoista tai viisumivelvollisuudesta. Varmista ajantasaiset maahantulosäädökset kohdemaan viranomaisilta, esimerkiksi maan lähimmästä edustustosta. Lue lisää Suomen kansalaisen matkustusasiakirjoista osoitteessa https://um.fi/passi/

themes.heading: Gesundheit
themes.text: Krankenhäuser entsprechen nicht dem europäischen Standard. Es besteht kein Sozialversicherungsabkommen mit Österreich. Es wird darauf hingewiesen, dass die Kosten einer allfälligen Krankenhausbehandlung erheblich sein können. Der Abschluss einer alle Risiken abdeckenden Reiseversicherung wird daher vor Reiseantritt dringend empfohlen. Die Mitnahme einer Reiseapotheke, die nicht nur regelmäßig benötigte Arzneimittel, sondern auch Medikamente für gängige Reiseerkrankungen beinhaltet, wird empfohlen. Wer auf bestimmte Medikamente angewiesen ist, sollte einen ausreichenden Vorrat und einen Nachweis über die ärztliche Verschreibung mitnehmen, auf der Homepage des Sozialministeriums finden Sie nähere Informationen zur Mitnahme von Medikamenten ins Ausland. Es wird empfohlen, rechtzeitig vor Reisebeginn den Hausarzt oder eine andere geeignete Einrichtung zu kontaktieren, um sich über eventuell erforderliche Reiseimpfungen zu erkundigen.

themes.heading: Safety
themes.text: Theft, armed assault, kidnapping and other violent crimes occur, including in tourist areas and on public transport. Secure your valuables. If attacked or robbed, don't resist. Avoid large public gatherings and protests. These can turn violent. They may also lead to roadblocks. Don't cross roadblocks, even if they're unattended. Be alert when travelling to interior parts of the country, including San Pedro, Concepcion and Canindeyu. Full travel advice: Safety

themes.heading: Health
themes.text: Yellow fever is a risk and can be fatal. Get vaccinated before you travel. Other insect-borne diseases, including zika virus, chikungunya and dengue, are also present. Take precautions and use insect repellent. Foodborne, waterborne and other infectious diseases are prevalent. These include hepatitis A, typhoid and rabies. Check your vaccinations are up to date before you travel. Boil drinking water or drink bottled water. Avoid ice cubes. Hantavirus is a risk in Paraguay. It's generally spread through contact with infected rodents. Avoid contact with live or dead rodents, nests, burrows and droppings. Full travel advice: Health

themes.heading: Local laws
themes.text: Paraguay has severe penalties for drug offences. These include prison sentences and heavy fines. Don't use or carry illegal drugs. Other illegal activities include importing or exporting firearms, medications, toys that look like weapons and protected species. There are no restrictions on same-sex relationships but same-sex marriages aren't recognised. Paraguay doesn't recognise Australian-Paraguayan dual nationality. Our ability to provide consular assistance to Australians who also hold a Paraguayan passport may be limited. Full travel advice: Local laws

themes.heading: Travel
themes.text: Australian passport holders don't require visitor visas to enter Paraguay on a regular passport for stays up to 90 days. If you're travelling on a different type of passport, check entry requirements with the nearest embassy or consulate of Paraguay. Entry and exit conditions can change at short notice. You should contact the nearest embassy or consulate of Paraguay for the latest details. Counterfeit bank notes are common. Use only registered banks or exchange bureaus to change money. Carry identification with you. You may need to show it when paying with a credit or debit card. Use only registered taxi companies or ride-hailing apps which keep a record of drivers. Book taxis at major hotels or by phone, especially after dark. Some unregistered taxis may appear to be official. Passengers have been robbed in unregistered taxis. Full travel advice: Travel

themes.heading: Local contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas. Australia has an Honorary Consul in Asuncion. The consulate provides limited consular and passport services. The office is open part-time. You need to make an appointment. You can get full consular and passport assistance from the Australian Embassy in Argentina. Full travel advice: Local contacts

themes.heading: Crime
themes.text: Violent crime occurs, including in the capital city of Asunción and tourist areas such as San Pedro and Concepción. Incidents can involve armed assaults, car thefts, and kidnappings. Thieves on motorbikes sometimes steal from pedestrians or threaten them with weapons. Pickpocketing and other forms of petty crime are common in major cities and on public transport. Hotspots for petty crime include Asunción, Ciudad del Este, and Pedro Juan Caballero. If you are attacked or robbed, don't resist, as muggers can be violent and may be armed. Transnational criminal organisations carry out drug trafficking and arms smuggling, particularly along Paraguay’s eastern border with Brazil. This is most prevalent from Pedro Juan Caballero south to Ciudad del Este, including the Tri-Border Area of Paraguay, Brazil and Argentina. These organisations have carried out violent attacks in these north-eastern provinces as well as Asuncion. While tourists have not been specifically targeted, bystanders could still be at risk. To protect yourself from crime: be alert in tourist locations, crowded public places and downtown areas avoid walking after dark only use ATMs in controlled areas such as banks and shopping malls and avoid using them after dark ask your hotel about areas to avoid downtown ensure your belongings are secure at all times, particularly when using public transport use registered taxis keep car windows closed, doors locked at all times and keep valuables out of sight do not leave valuables in your car, including in the boot.

themes.heading: Kidnapping
themes.text: Kidnapping can happen anywhere, even in places that seem safe. High-profile kidnappings have occurred in areas where the group known as the Ejercito del Pueblo Paraguayo (EPP) or Army of the Paraguayan People, operates. The EPP operates in the northeast, Concepción and San Pedro. Foreigners living in these areas could be targeted. The Australian Government's longstanding policy is that it doesn't make payments or concessions to kidnappers. More information, including advice on how to reduce your risk of kidnapping: Kidnapping

themes.heading: Civil unrest and political tension
themes.text: Demonstrations and protests occur frequently, usually in downtown Asuncion near government offices and can lead to roadblocks and road closures causing delays. Public protests and large events can turn violent. To protect yourself: avoid protests and demonstrations don't cross roadblocks monitor the media for updates follow the advice of local authorities More information: Demonstrations and civil unrest

themes.heading: Terrorism
themes.text: Terrorism is a threat worldwide. Terrorist attacks could occur anywhere and at any time. Be alert to possible threats, especially in public places. Terrorists may target tourist areas or other places frequented by foreigners and where large crowds may gather. Possible targets include: places of worship or religious holidays hotels, clubs, restaurants and cafes markets and shopping malls outdoor recreation events, including festivals, concerts, and sporting venues public transport, airports and railway hubs To stay safe: report suspicious activities or items to the police check the media for new threats take official warnings seriously follow the instructions of local authorities If there’s an attack, leave the area as soon as it's safe to do so. More information: Terrorism

themes.heading: Extreme weather and natural disasters
themes.text: The rainy season is from December to March. Heavy rains can disrupt public transport schedules. Floods can occur. Monitor regional weather forecasts to plan your travel. If a natural disaster occurs, follow the advice of local authorities. More information: Global Disaster Alert and Coordination System

themes.heading: Travel insurance
themes.text: Get comprehensive travel insurance before you leave and closely read the Product Disclosure Statement (PDS) for what is and isn’t covered. It must cover all medical costs overseas, including evacuation. The Australian Government will not pay for these costs. If you can’t afford travel insurance, you shouldn’t travel. This is true for everyone, even if you are healthy and fit. If you don’t have insurance, you might have to pay thousands of dollars up front for medical care. Before you travel, check your policy, make sure: you know what activities and medical care your insurance covers, including evacuations your insurance covers you for your whole trip, including any stopovers you know any exclusions – for example, you might need to pay extra for activities like motorcycling, snow sports, scuba diving or bungee jumping. More information: Travel insurance

themes.heading: Physical and mental health
themes.text: Travelling overseas can be stressful and uncertain. You may experience unfamiliar environments, travel disruptions, changes in normal routines and limited access to health care. Not taking your prescribed medications is a common cause of mental ill-health for Australians overseas. If you are concerned about your welfare, or someone else's, contact your nearest Australian embassy, high commission, or consulate. You can also call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on +61 2 6261 3305. Mental health resources: Information and advice on mental illness (HealthDirect). Living with mental illness (BeyondBlue) Medicare Mental Health (the Department of Health and Aged Care) More information: General health advice Mental health support overseas

themes.heading: Insect-borne diseases
themes.text: There's a risk of yellow fever in Paraguay. Yellow fever is a potentially fatal virus spread by mosquitoes. It's prevented by vaccination. Get vaccinated before you travel. Zika virus is also common across Paraguay. If you're pregnant, the Australian Department of Health and Aged Care recommends that you: discuss travel plans with your doctor consider deferring non-essential travel to affected areas Other common insect-borne disease risks include: dengue chikungunya Insect-borne disease outbreaks have occurred in Asuncion, Concepcion and Ciudad del Este. To protect yourself from disease: make sure your accommodation is insect-proof use insect repellent wear long, loose, light-coloured clothing

themes.heading: Other health risks
themes.text: Waterborne, foodborne and other infectious diseases are common. These include, but are not limited to: typhoid hepatitis A rabies To protect yourself from illness: drink boiled water or bottled water with sealed lids avoid ice cubes avoid raw and undercooked food, such as salads avoid bathing in fresh water, such as lakes and rivers make sure your vaccinations are up to date avoid contact with dogs and other mammals If you're bitten or scratched by an animal, get medical help straight away. Seek medical advice if you suspect poisoning or have a fever or diarrhoea. More information: Infectious diseases

themes.heading: Medical facilities
themes.text: Medical care is adequate in major cities but is limited in smaller cities and rural areas. Hospitals require an up-front payment or proof of medical insurance before they'll treat you, even in an emergency.

themes.heading: Drugs
themes.text: Penalties for drug offences are severe, including mandatory prison sentences and heavy fines. More information: Carrying or using drugs

themes.heading: LGBTQIA+ travellers
themes.text: Same-sex relationships are legal but same-sex marriages are not recognised. People could be discriminated against based on their same-sex relationships, gender identity, gender expression, or sex characteristics. More information: Advice for LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Visas and border measures
themes.text: Each country or territory decides its own entry and exit rules, including health border screening measures. To find out what you need to enter a destination, ask their embassy, consulate or immigration office.

themes.heading: Visitor visas
themes.text: Australian passport holders don't require visitor visas to enter Paraguay when they travel on a regular passport for up to 90 days. Ensure your passport is stamped, signed, and dated by immigration when entering. If you don't, you'll have to pay a fine when you leave. Entry and exit conditions can change. Contact the nearest embassy or consulate of Paraguay for details about visas, currency, customs and other travel requirements. For more information on the destination's entry/exit requirements, check Paraguay’s National Directorate of Immigration’s website.

themes.heading: Yellow fever vaccination
themes.text: You may need a valid yellow fever vaccination certificate to enter. Some airlines may want to see one when you leave. Check the yellow fever entry requirements by contacting the nearest embassy or consulate of Paraguay or visiting Paraguay’s National Directorate of Immigration’s website. Find out about returning to Australia after exposure to yellow fever. More information: Countries with a risk of yellow fever

themes.heading: Travel via the United States
themes.text: If you're travelling to or from Paraguay through the US, you must meet US entry or transit requirements. More information: Embassy or consulate of Paraguay Travel advice for the US

themes.heading: Travelling to Brazil from Paraguay
themes.text: Read our travel advice for Brazil. Australians need a visa to enter Brazil. For more information, contact the embassy or consulate of Brazil.

themes.heading: Travelling with children
themes.text: Children under the age of 18, with a birthplace or residency in Paraguay, must leave Paraguay with a legalised travel authorisation from any non-accompanying parent(s). A birth certificate may be required to verify the parents. If one of the parents is deceased, a death certificate will need to accompany the birth certificate. Check with the nearest embassy or consulate of Paraguay about the required documents for minors travelling to/from Paraguay and how to obtain them. More information: Advice for people travelling with children

themes.heading: Lost or stolen passport
themes.text: Your passport is valuable and can be used by others to commit crimes. Don’t let anyone trick you into giving it to them. Always keep it in a safe place. If your passport is lost or stolen, tell the Australian Government: In Australia: contact the Australian Passport Information Service. Overseas: contact the nearest Australian embassy or consulate.

themes.heading: Passport with ‘X’ gender identifier
themes.text: Australian passports comply with international standards for sex and gender. If your passport has an 'X' in the sex field, we can’t guarantee your entry or transit to other countries. Ask the embassy or consulate of your destinations if they’ll accept your passport with an 'X' gender marker. Check all transit destinations too. More information: LGBTQIA+ travellers

themes.heading: Money
themes.text: The currency of Paraguay is the Guarani (PYG). Declare all amounts over USD $10,000 (or its foreign equivalent) on arrival and departure. Failing to do so is a violation and can result in the seizure and forfeiture of your money. Use registered banks or an exchange bureau to change money. Avoid changing money with people on the streets. Counterfeit notes are common. You may need to show ID when paying with a credit or debit card.

themes.heading: Travelling to Iguazu Falls
themes.text: If you plan to visit Iguazu Falls, read our travel advice for: Argentina Brazil Be cautious when crossing the tri-border area between Argentina, Brazil and Paraguay, as criminal activities are common. Avoid crossing the borders after dark. If you intend to make a day trip to Brazil, make sure you meet the entry/visa requirements.

themes.heading: Road travel
themes.text: Driving in Paraguay can be dangerous. The network of sealed roads in Paraguay is limited. Heavy rains can make some roads impassable. If you plan to drive in Paraguay: you will need an international driving permit check you have adequate travel insurance learn local traffic laws and practices avoid driving at night in rural areas More information: Driving or riding

themes.heading: Taxis
themes.text: Taxis are often in poor condition and many don't have functioning seat belts. Some taxis may appear to be official but aren't registered. There are reports of passengers being robbed in unregistered taxis. If you need to take a taxi: use only registered taxi companies or ride-hailing apps which are available in major cities after dark, book registered taxis by phone or at major hotels

themes.heading: Air travel
themes.text: DFAT doesn’t give advice about how safe different airlines or flight paths are. Safety risks and weather events may cause flight delays and cancellations. Contact your travel agent or airline to confirm your plans. Airlines and airports can restrict what you can take in your luggage. Regulations may be different to those in Australia. Check with your airline before you travel. Check Paraguay's air safety profile with the Aviation Safety Network. More information: Air travel

themes.heading: Consular contacts
themes.text: The Australian Government aims to provide high quality consular assistance. There are limits to what we can do. Read the Consular Services Charter to understand how we can assist you overseas.

themes.heading: Australian Consulate, Asuncion

Santisimo Sacramento 1026 Esquina Roque Centurion Edificio Eteria (4th floor ‘’Fundacion Moises Bertoni’’) Asuncion, Paraguay Phone: +595 985 191 000 The Consulate is headed by an Honorary Consul and can provide limited consular and passport services. The office is open part-time. You need to make an appointment. You can also get consular and passport assistance from the Australian Embassy in Argentina.

themes.text: Villanueva 1400, (C1426BMJ) Buenos Aires, Argentina Phone: +54 11 4779 3500 Website: www.argentina.embassy.gov.au Facebook: Australia in Argentina, Uruguay and Paraguay X: @EmbAustraliaBA Instagram: ausemb_ar Check the Embassy website for details about opening hours and any temporary closures.

themes.heading: 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre
themes.text: In a consular emergency, if you can't contact an embassy, call the 24-hour Consular Emergency Centre on: +61 2 6261 3305 from overseas 1300 555 135 in Australia

themes.heading: 기본정보 (basisinformatie)
themes.text: 기본정보 ㅇ 정식국명 : 파라과이 공화국 ㅇ 위치 : 브라질, 아르헨티나, 볼리비아와 접경하고 있는 남미 내륙국 ㅇ 국토면적 : 406,752 km²(남한의 4배, 한반도의 1.8배) ㅇ 수도 : Asuncion ㅇ 공식언어 : 스페인어 및 과라니어 공용 ㅇ 종교 : 공식 국교는 없으며, 종교의 자유 인정 - 전 인구의 90% 카톨릭 신자 정치현황 ㅇ 정부형태 : 대통령제(임기5년, 단임제) ㅇ 주요인사 ㅇ 대통령 : 산티아고 페냐(Santiago Peña)('23.08.15 취임) ㅇ 외교장관 : 루벤 라미레스(Rubén Ramirez)('23.08.15 취임) ㅇ 의회구성 : 양원제 ㅇ 상원 : 45석(임기 5년) ㅇ 하원 : 80석(임기 5년) 경제현황 ㅇ GDP : 411억$ (2022) ㅇ 1인당GDP : 5525$ (2022) ㅇ 경제성장률 : 0.2% (2022) ㅇ 교역 : 2022 - 수출액 : 94억 6000만$ - 수입액 : 79억 1000만$

themes.heading: 사건ㆍ사고의 유형
themes.text: [무장총기강도] ㅇ 개인주택, 부재중인 아파트또는 상가에 단체로 침입하여 현금 등 금품을 강탈하는 사례 발생 [노상강도] ㅇ 해질 무렵 또는 늦은 저녁시간대 오토바이를 타고 다니면서 혼자 길을 걸어가는 행인들을 총기 또는 흉기로 위협, 금품 및 휴대폰 등 소지품을 강탈하는 사례가 최근 급증 [소매치기, 소지품 탈취범] ㅇ 시내버스 또는 노상에서 일명 ‘까발요 로꼬(미친 말)’이라 불리는 소매치기들에 의해 소지품(주로 귀금속 및 휴대폰, 디지털 카메라 등 고가 전자제품)이 강탈당하는 사례가 빈번히 발생 ㅇ 사람들이 대중교통 수단을 많이 이용하는 출퇴근 시간대에는 버스안에서 은밀히 흉기로 위협, 금품을 강탈하는 사례도 있음

themes.heading: 자연재해
themes.text: ㅇ 파라과이는 중남미 중심부에 위치한 내륙국가로서, 지형적 특성상 전 국토가 거의 평지이며, 태평양 연안국가들에서 주로 발생하는 해일, 산사태 등과 같은 대규모의 자연재해가 없으며, 위협감을 느낄 정도의 지진발생 사례도 전무 ㅇ 그러나, 특정 저지대 지방의 경우 폭우가 계속되거나 우기(10-11월)에는 홍수로 인해 일부 지역이 침수되는 등 피해가 극심하고(도로가 물에 잠겨 통행이 불가할 정도), 산이 없어 천둥ㆍ번개가 칠 때 낙뢰로 사망자가 다수 발생함으로 동 시기에 저지대로의 여행은 자제하는 것이 바람직함 ㅇ 무더운 더위가 지속되는 계절에는 모기를 매체로 전염되는 뎅기열병 및 황열병이 창궐한 사례가 있으므로 모기에 물리지 않도록 특별한 주의가 필요

themes.heading: 유의해야할 지역
themes.text: ㅇ 외국인 상대 소매치기, 강탈 위험 지역: Centro 빈민촌, Lambare 빈민촌: 특히 Centro 시장은 넓고 복잡하므로 쇼핑시 인적이 드문 깊숙한 골목으로 진입은 자제 요망 ㅇ 한인동포들이 주로 거주하는 4시장(Mercado Cuatro)에도 소매치기와 노상강도 주의 ㅇ 국경도시인 Ciudad del Este와 Encarnacion에서도 노상강도 피해 자주 발생하므로 주의 요망 ㅇ San Pedro, Concepcion지역: 불법무장단체인 국민연합군(EPP) 출몰이 빈번한 지역으로써 기업인 상대로 피랍, 소규모 테러를 자행하는 바, 동 지역 여행 자제 요망

themes.heading: 출입국 (in- en uitreis)
themes.text: 무비자 입국 가능 여부(1회 입국시 최대 연속 체류 가능 일자) ㅇ 외교관 여권 소지자 : O, (90일) 협정 ㅇ 관용여권 소지자 : O, (90일) 협정 ㅇ 일반여권 소지자(복수와 단수를 구분할 경우 별도 표기 필요) : O, (30일) 상호주의 ㅇ 도착사증 발급 여부 : 도착사증 미발급 입국시 유의사항 등 파라과이 보건복지부는 결의안 제268/2025호에 따라 입국 및 출국 요건으로 국제황열병예방접종증명서 제출을 의무화하고 있는 바, 파라과이를 방문하고자 하시는 분은 아래 내용을 참고하시기 바랍니다. 🦟 황열별 예방접종 ㅇ 증명서 제출 의무 대상자: - 위험지역 출신 외국인: 볼리비아, 브라질, 콜롬비아, 가이아나, 페루 - 위험지역으로 여행하거나 귀국하는 파라과이 국민 및 파라과이 거주 외국인 📌 국제 황열 예방접종증명서는 종이 또는 디지털 형식 모두 인정됩니다. 💉 예방접종은 여행 최소 10일 전에 접종해야 효과가 보장됩니다. ❌ 제외 대상자 아래 대상자는 증명서 제출 의무가 면제됩니다: 👶 1세 미만 영아 및 👵 59세 초과 고령자 🩺 의학적 사유로 예방접종이 금기된 자 ○해당자는 의사 또는 출신국 보건당국이 발행한 진단서로 입증해야 하며, ○입국 후 6일간 보건당국의 건강 모니터링을 받아야 합니다. ✈️ 위험지역을 경유(24시간 이내 체류)하는 환승객 🚫 증명서 미소지 시 조치 ■ 황열병 예방접종증명서를 소지하지 않은 외국인은 입국이 불허됩니다. ■접종 후 10일이 경과하지 않은 경우에도 입국이 거부됩니다. ■위험지역 출신이나 타국에 거주 중인 외국인은 거주지를 증명하는 공식 서류를 제시하면 입국 허용됩니다. 🆘 예외적 입국 허가 다음과 같은 특수 사유에 해당하는 경우, 보건감시국(Dirección General de Vigilancia de la Salud)의 사전 허가를 조건으로 입국이 허용됩니다: ■인도적 사유 ■상업 활동 ■전문 기술인력 ■항공기 승무원 ■기타 정부가 필요하다고 인정하는 경우 👉 입국 후 6일간 보건당국의 건강 관찰 대상이 됩니다. 출처: https://www.mspbs.gov.py/portal/33333/requisitos-sanitarios-para-ingreso-a-paraguay.html ㅇ 본인이 복용하던 약을 가지고 주재국 입국 시 휴대품 검사 등에서 문제 삼는 경우는 별로 많지 않으나, 이 경우에도 발행된 의사의 처방전 휴대 시 큰 문제 없음 (※ 가급적 영문 처방전 또는 스페인어 처방전 번역본 지참 권유) 출입국시 유의사항 등 ㅇ 출입국 도장을 반드시 확인 권고 ㅇ 출입국시 확인도장을 찍어주지 않는 경우가 간혹 발생하므로, 본인이 반드시 확인할 것을 당부 드림 ㅇ 미성년자 단독 입국의 경우, 법정대리인인 부모의 미동반 여행동의서, 가족관계증명서, 부모의 여권 사본(※스페인어 공증 필요) 등이 필요하므로 주의 당부 참고자료 ㅇ 해당 국가의 주한공관홈페이지 : www.embaparcorea.org ㅇ 해당국가의 한국 대사관 홈페이지 : pry.mofa.go.kr ㅇ 기타 참고자료 - 해당국 외교부 : www.mre.gov.py - 해당국 이민청 : www.migraciones.gov.py

themes.heading: 일반문화
themes.text: ㅇ 여성과 이성끼리 양쪽볼에 가볍게 입을 맞추며 인사함 ㅇ 남성끼리는 가벼운 악수

themes.heading: 종교관련
themes.text: ㅇ 파라과이는 전통적 가톨릭 국가로 전제 국민의 약 90% 상당이 가톨릭 신자이나, 종교의 자유가 보장되고 있으며 파라과이의 국민들은 여타 종교에 대해 별다른 선입견 없음 ㅇ 그러나 가톨릭 성당 방문시에는 단정한 복장을 하는 것이 바람직하며, 예배당 내에서 큰소리 대화를 나누거나 소란을 피우는 등의 행동은 삼가

themes.heading: 주요 교통 법규 및 문화
themes.text: ㅇ 파라과이 운전자들은 속도․신호 위반 등 교통법규를 지키지 않는 경우가 있고, 운전시 잘 양보하지 않아 교차로에서는 일단 정차하고 좌우를 확인하고 통과하는 것이 바람직함 - (파라과이는 주요도로를 제외하고 신호없는 교차로가 대부분으로) 특히 신호없는 교차로에서 ‘PARE’라는 표지판이 있는 경우, 이는 우리말로 ‘우선멈춤’이라는 의미로, 반드시 정차후 좌우를 확인 후 교차로를 통과하여여 함 ㅇ 국제운전면허증은 1년 이내에 현지에서 유효

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 사례
themes.text: ㅇ 운전자의 안전의식 부족 및 도로상 횡단보도․보행자용 신호등과 같은 교통안전시설이 거의 없어 보행자 교통사고 위험이 매우 높은 편으로 주의 요망 - 따라서 길을 건널 때에는 좌우를 잘 살펴 다가오는 차량이 있는지를 확인후 건너가는 것이 바람직

themes.heading: 관련 사건사고 발생시 대처방법
themes.text: ㅇ 교통사고 발생시 경찰에 신고 및 재외공관 긴급연락처로 연락 ㅇ 만일의 사고로 의식을 잃었을 때를 대비하여 주변사람들이 신원을 확인할 수 있도록 신분증 또는 여권사본 등을 주머니에 소지하는 것이 바람직 [대중교통] ㅇ 우버, Bolt 플랫폼 사용 가능(현금 지불 선호) ㅇ 시내버스: 버스 요금은 2,300-3,400 과라니(원화 약 500원)이며, 노선 안내가 되어있지 않아 외국인이 이용하기에는 불편함 ㅇ 택시는 콜택시제 및 택시정류소 운영을 하고 있으며, 각 지역마다 택시정류소가 설치되어 정류소 전화로 택시를 부를 수 있음 ㅇ 택시 차량색깔은 주로 노란색으로 통일되어 있으나, 시우닫 델 에스떼시와 같이 백색인 지역도 있는 등 지역에 따라 상이할 수 있음 ㅇ 요금제는 시간, 거리 병산제 (일요일 및 휴일, 야간 할증료 30% 추가) ㅇ 기본요금은 5,000과라니(원화 약 천원) ㅇ 수도 아순시온 콜택시 전화번호 021-311-080

themes.heading: 기타 유의사항
themes.text: ㅇ 교통안내 표지판 등 기본시설이 미비하고, 일방통행 도로가 많아 운전시 각별한 주의가 필요 ㅇ 도로는 차선이 희미하고 노면이 잘 정비되어 있지 않은 편이며, 주요 도로 이외에는 비포장이 많아 차량 마모가 심한 편임

themes.heading: 날씨
themes.text: ㅇ 연평균 기온: 22도 (지역에 따라 극심한 편차) ㅇ 하계 (10~3월): 22 ~42도 (평균 32도) ㅇ 동계 (4~9월): 3~32도 (평균 15도) ㅇ 수도권지역 하계 평균기온: 34도 ㅇ 연간 평균강우량: 1,500mm (브라질과의 국경지대는 1,700mm)

themes.heading: 기타
themes.text: ㅇ 시내버스: 버스 요금은 2,300 과라니(원화 약 500원)이며, 노선 안내가 되어있지 않아 외국인이 이용하기에는 불편합니다. 소매치기, 들치기 등 금품강탈 위험이 있으므로, 현지 사정을 잘 모르는 경우에는 택시를 이용하는것이 좋습니다. ㅇ 택시 : 콜택시제 및 택시정류소 체계로 운영되고 있으며, 각 지역마다 택시정류소가 설치되어 있으며, 정류소로 전화하여 택시를 부를수 있습니다. 수도권지역 택시들은 대부분 노란색이고, 국경지역에는 하얀색입니다. 거의 모든 택시기사들과의 영어대화가 불가능함으로 이동장소 및 주소를 미리 숙지하신 후 탑승하시는게 바람직합니다. 택시요금은 미터제로 운영되고 있습니다. ㅇ 주요 도로이외에는 비포장이 많아 차량 마모가 심한 편입니다. 교통안내 표지판 등 기본 시설이 미비하고 일방통행 도로가 많아 운전시 각별한 주의가 필요합니다. 현지인들은 과속, 신호위반 등 교통법규를 잘 지키지 않는 편이며, 잘 양보하지 않아 교차로나 골목길에서는 일단 정차하시는 것이 좋습니다. 국제운전면허증은 관할기관에서 인증을 받은 후 1년동안 유효하나 이후에는 체류자격과 함께 관할 시청에서 현지 운전면허증을 발급받아야 합니다. ㅇ 파라과이는 국내선 항공이 발달되어 있지 않으며, Ciudad del Este로만 하루 1편이 운행되고 있습니다.(다른 지방도시는 항공편이 없습니다.)
